blockReason: Visual QA stayed below 95/100 after 3 iterations (best score: 88). Defects: Repetitive phrasing in job descriptions reduces scanning efficiency; The job roles block is visually dense and could benefit from improved spacing; The visual hierarchy could be slightly more candidate-first by emphasizing the job roles more than the project title.; The interview dates layout is somewhat tight and could benefit from more spacing for better readability.; Text density is quite high but well organized for newspaper archetype; The QR code looks slightly detached and could integrate better in design; Headline 'Bilfinger Shutdown Project' could have slightly more visual emphasis to dominate more

defects: Repetitive phrasing in job descriptions reduces scanning efficiency
The job roles block is visually dense and could benefit from improved spacing

requiredCorrections.note: Add more spacing between job roles to reduce crowding and improve readability in the job listing block.
Consider rephrasing the repeated phrase 'Experience in shutdown projects mandatory' to improve scanning and reduce redundancy.

defects: The visual hierarchy could be slightly more candidate-first by emphasizing the job roles more than the project title.
The interview dates layout is somewhat tight and could benefit from more spacing for better readability.

requiredCorrections.note: Add more spacing around the interview date boxes to improve readability and avoid crowding.
Make the job roles stand out slightly more to pass the first-second attention test and reinforce candidate-first hierarchy.

defects: Text density is quite high but well organized for newspaper archetype
The QR code looks slightly detached and could integrate better in design
Headline 'Bilfinger Shutdown Project' could have slightly more visual emphasis to dominate more

requiredCorrections.note: Enhance visual integration of the QR code element to feel more designed-in and less like a sticker.
Increase prominence of 'Bilfinger Shutdown Project' headline to make it the strongest visual focus after the agency name.
